description: Manufacturer-specific rules for identifying Indoor Unit (IDU) and Outdoor Unit (ODU) components in BAFA heat pump product registrations. Rules are evaluated in priority order within each manufacturer; first matching rule wins. Confidence scores reflect external manufacturer validation performed 2026-06-21; v2.2.0 schema migration 2026-06-23: VAI-002 role reversal, SAM-001/SAM-002 split, WLF-001 disabled, source_type corrections. v2.3.0 2026-06-23: WLF manufacturer_contains case-bug fixed (Wolf→WOLF), WLF-002 added (20 monoblock), WLF-002→WLF-003 (15 slash), VIE-003 added (85 AWO monoblock). v2.4.0 2026-06-23: LGE-002 regex space-bug fixed (+7 HM monoblock hits), PAN-002 bafa_count corrected (66→7), PAN-003 added (59 bracket-slash split), BUD-002 added (33 MB monoblock+hydraulic module), NIB-002 added (16 standalone AMS/F-series), STI-002 added (18 WPL-A monoblock), WEI-002 added (4 WAB monoblock). Generator: bracket_slash_pan + paren_plus_buderus extractors added; hydraulic_module_model field now populated from extraction. v2.5.0 2026-06-23: MIT-002 added (713 ECODAN plus-separator split), MIT-003 added (3 PUHZ-W monoblock), MIT-004 added (6 PUZ-WZ standalone outdoor), LGE-003 added (4 HM+HN split), LGE-004 added (6 standalone HM monoblock), SAM-003 added (29 WPLW-Mono), SAM-004 added (4 WPLW-Split), VAI-004 added (10 VWL outdoor), BUD-003 added (6 nested paren MB+hydraulic), BUD-004 added (6 MBE+ nested paren&hydraulic). Generator: paren_plus_buderus_nested + paren_amp_buderus_nested extractors added.
schema_notes: classification values: confirmed_set | confirmed_not_set | standalone_odu | variant_label | requires_research | unclassified
confidence_score: 0.00–1.00. Internal threshold: 0.90 (high confidence). Display threshold: 0.95.
extraction_method: key into the script extract() function map; 'none' = extraction not possible.
manufacturer_contains: substring match against the BAFA manufacturer field.
model_match.type: 'contains_all' = all patterns must appear; 'contains_any' = at least one; 'regex' = single regex test.
model_exclude: if set, products matching this are skipped by the rule.
bafa_count_approx: approximate count in the 2026-06 BAFA master seed snapshot.

confidence_cap_policy.bafa_pattern_analysis_only_other_classif: cap 0.88 if pattern is self-evident; else 0.85
confidence_cap_policy.self_describing_bafa_name_literal: cap 0.98 (MONOBLOCK / Set / IDU+ODU literal present in BAFA name)

rules.label: Viessmann — explicit IDU/ODU labels

evidence.title: BAFA registration name contains literal 'IDU-A' and 'ODU' prefixes
evidence.what_it_proves: Viessmann Vitocal 250/300 AWOT registrations embed 'IDU-A [model]' and 'ODU [model]' directly in the BAFA product name. The classification is self-certifying from the registration text.

rules.label: Viessmann — Vitocal AWO outdoor monoblock main unit
rules.confidence_cap_applied: self_describing: AWO = Außen-Wärmepumpe Outdoor, unambiguously outdoor-installed monoblock

evidence.title: BAFA model name contains AWO/AWOT/HAWO — Außen-Wärmepumpe Outdoor designation
evidence.what_it_proves: Viessmann Vitocal 150-A/151-A/200-A/222-A/250-A/250-AH/252-A series use 'AWO' (Außen-Wärmepumpe Outdoor) or 'HAWO' (Hochtemperatur-AWO) in the type designation, indicating an outdoor-installed air-to-water heat pump with no separate indoor unit. AWO = monoblock outdoor main by manufacturer naming convention. Captures both slash-variant (AWO-E-AC/AWO-E-AC-AF) and single-entry (AWO 201.A09) model formats. Confidence 0.92: designation is manufacturer-convention self-describing; no standalone Viessmann PDF mapping document consulted.

rules.notes: Rule placed before VIE-002 to capture AWO-containing slash products that would otherwise be variant_label. Covers Vitocal 150-A/151-A (AF-suffix slash variants) and Vitocal 200-A/222-A/250-A/250-AH/252-A (no slash). AWB/AWBT/HAWB products excluded — require further research to confirm outdoor-only vs split-set classification.

rules.label: Viessmann — slash variant codes (not ODU/IDU, not AWO)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only pattern: capped at 0.85
rules.notes: After VIE-003 captures AWO slash products, VIE-002 receives remaining non-AWO slash products (AWB/AWBT series, 14 products).

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Viessmann slash = antifreeze variant
evidence.what_it_proves: Viessmann registers antifreeze-option variants as 'AWO-E-AC/AWO-E-AC-AF'. Both segments share the same base code with an -AF suffix — not an ODU/IDU separator. No external source confirmed.

rules.label: Panasonic — bracket-plus split set [ODU + IDU]

evidence.title: Panasonic Aquarea Installation Manual — WH-UD outdoor / WH-ADC indoor
evidence.what_it_proves: Official Panasonic EU/UK installation manual explicitly labels WH-UD as 'OUTDOOR UNIT' and WH-ADC as 'indoor unit of the bi-bloc system'. Bracket-plus BAFA notation '[WH-UD... + WH-ADC...]' confirmed as ODU + IDU.

rules.label: Panasonic — single-bracket monoblock [WH-MDC...]

evidence.title: Panasonic Aquarea Monoblock Installation Manual — WH-MDC
evidence.what_it_proves: Official Panasonic documentation confirms WH-MDC series is a monoblock (all-in-one outdoor unit) with no separate indoor unit. Bracket-only BAFA notation '[WH-MDC...]' with no '+' separator confirms single-unit registration.

rules.label: Panasonic — bracket-slash split set [IDU / ODU]
rules.confidence_cap_applied: bafa_pattern_analysis_only_confirmed_set: role assignment (ADC=IDU, UDZ=ODU) inferred from PAN-001 evidence + capacity family matching; no standalone official Panasonic bracket-slash document consulted. Capped at 0.88.

evidence.title: Panasonic Aquarea Installation Manual — WH-UD outdoor / WH-ADC indoor (cross-applied to slash notation)
evidence.what_it_proves: PAN-001 evidence confirms WH-ADC = indoor hydrobox and WH-UD* = outdoor unit in Panasonic Aquarea systems. WH-UDZ has same 'UD' outdoor prefix. WH-SDC shares 'DC' hydrobox suffix with WH-ADC. Bracket-slash BAFA notation '[WH-ADC/SDC/SXC / WH-UDZ/WDG/UXZ]' places indoor code first, outdoor code second — consistent with one indoor paired with multiple outdoor capacity variants (UDZ03/05/07). Classification inferred by cross-referencing PAN-001 naming conventions; confidence capped at 0.88 (bafa_pattern_analysis_only cap for confirmed_set).

rules.example_model: Panasonic Aquarea H Gen Bi-bloc [WH-ADC0309K3E5 / WH-UDZ03KE5]
rules.notes: Applied after PAN-001 (bracket-plus) and PAN-002 (single-bracket WH-M). Bracket-slash products are bi-bloc split systems with IDU code first, ODU code second. slash separator ' / ' is space-padded in all observed BAFA examples.

rules.label: LG — THERMA V bracket-plus split set [HU + HN]

evidence.title: LG Therma V UK official documentation — THERMA SPLIT OUTDOOR/INDOOR labeling
evidence.what_it_proves: LG official UK documentation and product shipping labels explicitly label HU units as 'THERMA SPLIT OUTDOOR' and HN units as 'THERMA SPLIT INDOOR'. Confidence 0.92 (primary evidence from LG UK + distributor shipping labels, not a standalone LG-authored installation PDF).

rules.label: LG — THERMA V single-bracket monoblock [HM...]

evidence.title: LG THERMA V Monobloc — HM (HeatMonoblock) product family
evidence.what_it_proves: LG official UK documentation confirms HM prefix = HeatMonoblock, a single integrated outdoor unit with no separate indoor component. '[HM...]' in BAFA name with no '+' separator confirms single-unit registration.

rules.label: LG — THERMA V HM + HN plus-separator split package
rules.confidence_cap_applied: bafa_pattern_analysis_only_confirmed_set: HM+HN plus-separator role assignment inferred from BAFA name pattern and LG naming convention analogy. No dedicated LG EU documentation for this specific HM+HN bracket-free format found. Capped at 0.85.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — LG THERMA V HM + HN plus-separator format
evidence.what_it_proves: 4 BAFA products use 'THERMA V HMnnnHF + HNnnnnHY' notation without brackets. The ' + ' separator is consistent with other multi-component BAFA registrations. HM prefix (Heat Module) appears first (ODU position); HN prefix (Hydro Node/indoor unit) appears second (IDU position). Role assignment is inferred from the LG THERMA V naming convention where HM=outdoor-side, HN=indoor-side, analogous to the bracket-plus format captured by LGE-001.

rules.notes: Applied after LGE-001 (bracket-plus) and LGE-002 (single-bracket). The HM+HN non-bracket format is distinct from LGE-001 ([HU+HN] bracket) and LGE-002 ([HM] single bracket). The 'HF' and 'HY' suffixes indicate capacity/configuration variants. 4 products: HM071HF+HN1616HY, HM073HF+HN1639HY, HM091HF+HN1616HY, HM093HF+HN1639HY.

rules.label: LG — THERMA V HM standalone monoblock (no brackets, no +)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: self_describing: HM = HeatMonoblock per LGE-002 evidence. Products without brackets and without '+' are standalone monoblock units. Confidence 0.90.

evidence.title: LG THERMA V Monobloc — HM (HeatMonoblock) product family (cross-applied from LGE-002)
evidence.what_it_proves: LGE-002 confirms HM prefix = HeatMonoblock (single integrated outdoor unit). This rule captures the same HM monoblock family registered in a non-bracket format 'THERMA V HM051MRS.UA40' without the bracket notation used in LGE-002 '[HM...]'. The standalone (no brackets, no '+') format confirms a single-unit registration.

rules.notes: Applied after LGE-001/LGE-002/LGE-003. model_exclude for '[' prevents overlap with LGE-001/LGE-002 (bracket formats); model_exclude for '+' prevents overlap with LGE-003. 6 products: HM051/071/091MRS.UA40 and HM123/143/163MRS.UB40.

rules.label: ait-deutschland — bracket-plus split set [ODU + IDU]
rules.confidence_cap_applied: Partial external validation: LWAV as outdoor unit confirmed officially; LWUV as matched indoor module inferred from L-Split architecture, no dedicated official page found for LWUV prefix.

evidence.title: Alpha Innotec LWAV Series product page
evidence.what_it_proves: Alpha innotec official page states: 'Through the separation of outdoor and indoor units, valuable space can be saved inside the building' and 'The ready-to-plug-in connection concept of the outdoor unit makes it quick and easy to install.' LWAV is explicitly the outdoor unit of a split system requiring a separate indoor unit.

evidence.title: ManualsLib: Alpha Innotec L8 SPLIT / L12 SPLIT Installation Manual
evidence.what_it_proves: Manual covers L8 SPLIT/L12 SPLIT outdoor units + HM 8-12 SPLIT indoor hydraulic module as a two-component split system. Confirms ait-deutschland L-Split brand uses distinct outdoor + indoor module architecture.

rules.false_positive_notes: LWUV indoor module prefix not found on a dedicated official ait-deutschland page; inferred from L-Split system architecture. Bracket-plus notation consistent with Panasonic/LG OEM convention used by multiple manufacturers.

rules.label: FUJITSU GENERAL — bracket-plus split set [ODU + IDU] (Waterstage)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: Product-family rule confirmed (WO prefix = outdoor on current Fujitsu General EU product line). However, the specific BAFA model codes WOTAF140DD, WOTAHAN140DD, WGTAH14D6B were not found in any official Fujitsu General EU/DE documentation — likely discontinued pre-2015 Waterstage models. Cap at 0.85 (family pattern confirmed, specific codes unverifiable).

evidence.title: Fujitsu General EU product family — WO prefix = outdoor unit (current product line)
evidence.what_it_proves: Current Fujitsu General EU documentation confirms WO prefix = outdoor unit across the Waterstage product family (e.g., WOYG, WOYK series). General naming convention is validated. However, the specific BAFA codes WOTAF and WOTAHAN do not appear in any available official EU/DE Fujitsu General source.

rules.false_positive_notes: WOTAF140DD, WOTAHAN140DD, WGTAH14D6B not found in any current Fujitsu General official documentation. These are likely discontinued pre-2015 Waterstage models. The WO=outdoor rule is applied by family convention, not specific model confirmation. Manual review recommended for WOTAF/WOTAHAN BAFA entries.

rules.label: MTF — bracket-plus Samsung OEM split set [AE.../EU + AE.../EU]

evidence.title: MTF official DACH website — Samsung EHS exclusive distributor since 2003
evidence.what_it_proves: MTF official website confirms 'exklusiver Vertriebspartner Samsung seit 2003' (exclusive Samsung distribution partner since 2003). MTF products are Samsung EHS rebrands. MTF uses 'Außengerät' (outdoor) and 'Hydro-Einheit' (indoor) labeling consistent with Samsung EHS documentation. Bracket-plus notation confirms ODU + IDU split system.

rules.notes: Excludes MIM-E03EN products (handled by MTF-002). BAFA data has two spacing variants: MIM-E03EN and MIM- E03EN.

rules.label: MTF — WPLW-Mono + MIM-E03EN control box
rules.control_box_label: MIM-E03EN multi-zone interface module (control_box; NOT IDU)
rules.notes: MTF rebrands Samsung EHS Mono. MIM-E03EN is a multi-zone controller — NOT IDU. BAFA data has two spacing variants (MIM-E03EN and MIM- E03EN), handled by regex pattern. Split from MTF-001 in registry v2.1.0.

evidence.title: Samsung EHS service manual — MIM-E03 multi-zone interface module identification
evidence.what_it_proves: MIM-E03EN is the same Samsung multi-zone interface controller as MIM-E03FN/GN. It is a control_box, not an indoor hydrobox. MTF WPLW-Mono-RE products pair Samsung monoblock outdoor units with this controller for multi-zone heating.

rules.label: Buderus — paren-ampersand split set (ODU & IDU)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: capped at 0.85. No external Buderus/Bosch documentation was consulted to confirm which component is ODU vs IDU in the paren-ampersand notation.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Buderus paren-ampersand notation
evidence.what_it_proves: Buderus Logatherm WLW split systems use '(outdoor_model & indoor_model)' parenthetical notation. Pattern is consistent across all 6 registrations. First segment precedes '&'; second segment follows. Component role order inferred from BAFA pattern; no external URL validation performed.

rules.example_model: Buderus Logatherm WLW196i.2-8 AR E (WLW196i.2-8 AR-S.2 E & WLW196i.2 IK8 E)

rules.label: Buderus — paren-plus outdoor monoblock + hydraulic module (MB suffix)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: bafa_pattern_analysis_only: MB suffix is self-describing (Monoblock) for outdoor role; paren-plus structure with known Buderus WLW naming convention. Capped at 0.90 (self_describing evidence for MB prefix). deriveArchitecture resolves to monoblock_with_hydraulic_module.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Buderus paren-plus with MB monoblock suffix
evidence.what_it_proves: Buderus registers 'Logaplus M WLW176i-N AR E (Logatherm WLW-N MB AR + WLW176i-M E)' where MB suffix in the paren content indicates Monoblock outdoor unit. The second paren component (WLW176i-M E) is the indoor hydraulic station. Paren-plus structure distinct from BUD-001 ampersand format; model_exclude prevents overlap. ~33 products use this pattern in 2026-06 snapshot.

rules.example_model: Logaplus M WLW176i-10 AR E (Logatherm WLW-10 MB AR + WLW176i-12 E)
rules.notes: BUD-002 placed after BUD-001 (ampersand). model_exclude prevents double-matching with BUD-001 — ampersand products have '&' not '+'. extraction_method paren_plus_buderus: first segment → outdoor_unit_model, second segment → hydraulic_module_model.

rules.label: Buderus — nested paren MB plus monoblock + hydraulic module (W variant)
rules.hydraulic_module_label: WLW indoor hydraulic station/module (with (W) variant option)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: bafa_pattern_analysis_only: same classification logic as BUD-002 but with nested (W) variant option in the hydraulic module code. MB suffix is self-describing for outdoor role. Capped at 0.88. Uses paren_plus_buderus_nested extractor to handle one level of inner paren nesting.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Buderus WLW186i MB + (W) variant nested paren format
evidence.what_it_proves: 6 Buderus products use '... (W) (Logatherm WLW-N MB AR + WLW186i-12 E (W))' format where the inner paren group contains a nested '(W)' at the end of the hydraulic module code. BUD-002's [^()] character class fails to match the nested (W). paren_plus_buderus_nested tolerates one level of nested parens. Extraction: odu = 'Logatherm WLW-N MB AR', hydraulic_module = 'WLW186i-12 E (W)'.

rules.example_model: Logaplus M WLW186i-4 AR E (W) (Logatherm WLW-4 MB AR + WLW186i-12 E (W))
rules.notes: BUD-003 placed after BUD-002. The regex \(.*MB.*\+.*\(W\) specifically targets products with '(W)' inside the outer paren group. BUD-002 naturally fails these due to the nested paren issue.

rules.label: Buderus — MBE+ paren-ampersand monoblock + hydraulic module (nested W variant)
rules.odu_label: WLW-*MBE+ outdoor monoblock enhanced (MBE+ = Monoblock Enhanced)
rules.hydraulic_module_label: Logatherm WLW indoor hydraulic station (with (W) variant option)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: bafa_pattern_analysis_only: MBE+ suffix indicates enhanced monoblock outdoor unit. Ampersand separator analogous to BUD-001. Nested (W) inside paren group requires paren_amp_buderus_nested extractor. Capped at 0.88.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Buderus WLW186i MBE+ ampersand nested paren format
evidence.what_it_proves: 6 Buderus products use 'Logaplus WLW186i-N MBE+ AR (WLW-N MBE+ AR & Logatherm WLW186i.2 E (W))' format. MBE+ = MB Enhanced outdoor monoblock. The ampersand separates outdoor (WLW-N MBE+ AR) from indoor hydraulic module (Logatherm WLW186i.2 E (W)). The nested (W) breaks BUD-001's [^()] class. paren_amp_buderus_nested handles one level of nesting.

rules.example_model: Logaplus WLW186i-11 MBE+ AR E (WLW-11 MBE+ AR & Logatherm WLW186i.2 E (W))
rules.notes: 6 products: WLW186i-11/13/15 MBE+ AR E/TP variants. BUD-004 placed after BUD-003. paren_amp_buderus_nested: outer paren group split at ' & ', first segment → odu, second → hydraulic_module.

rules.label: Samsung Klimatechnik — monoblock outdoor + tank/indoor-side accessory (non-MIM)

evidence.title: Samsung EHS service manual — AE-BX/RX outdoor / AE-DN indoor identification
evidence.what_it_proves: Samsung service manual and official Samsung support page filename 'EHS_R-32_MONO_OUTDOOR_IM_EU' confirm AE-BX/RX/CX/DX are outdoor units. Samsung energy label confirms AE-DN is indoor hydrobox. Plus-separator notation 'AE[outdoor]+AE[indoor]' validated.

rules.false_positive_notes: FläktGroup embeds '+' within model option codes (e.g. FGAH2020AG1.SL+.E79) — prevented by manufacturer_contains match on 'Samsung Klimatechnik'.
rules.notes: Excludes MIM-E03 products — those are handled by SAM-002. idu_model currently holds tank code; will migrate to tank_model in next schema update. extraction_method updated to plus_separator_tank in v2.2.0 — second element now goes to tank_model, idu_model stays null.

rules.label: Samsung Klimatechnik — monoblock outdoor + MIM-E03 control box
rules.control_box_label: MIM-E03FN/GN multi-zone interface module (control_box; NOT IDU)
rules.notes: MIM-E03 is Samsung multi-zone interface controller — it is a control_box, not an IDU or hydrobox. idu_model is null for all SAM-002 products. control_box_model holds the MIM-E03 code. Split from SAM-001 in registry v2.1.0 per policy §14.2.

evidence.title: Samsung EHS service manual — MIM-E03 identified as multi-zone interface module
evidence.what_it_proves: Samsung official documentation confirms MIM-E03FN/GN is a Multi-zone Interface Module — a zone bridging controller with no heat exchanger. It is not an indoor hydrobox. Pairs with AE-BX/CX monoblock outdoor units as system controller.

rules.label: Samsung Klimatechnik — WPLW-Mono monoblock outdoor unit (self-describing name)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: self_describing: 'Mono' keyword in BAFA product name directly indicates monoblock configuration. Applied after SAM-001 (plus-separator) and SAM-002 (MIM-E03). Samsung WPLW-Hub Mono and WPLW-Hydro Mono and WPLW-Mono product families all carry the Mono designation. Confidence 0.90.

evidence.title: BAFA registration name contains literal 'Mono' keyword within WPLW- product family
evidence.what_it_proves: Samsung Klimatechnik registers monoblock heat pumps under WPLW- product families (WPLW-Hub Mono, WPLW-Hydro Mono, WPLW-Mono). The word 'Mono' is an explicit monoblock designation in the BAFA name. No '+' separator is present — these are single-unit registrations. The whole BAFA name functions as the outdoor monoblock product identifier.

rules.notes: 29 products: 8 WPLW-Hub Mono, 7 WPLW-Hydro Mono, 14 WPLW-Mono HT. All contain 'WPLW-' and 'Mono'. model classification as confirmed_not_set (outdoor_unit_model = item.model). Does not match WPLW-Split products (caught by SAM-004).

rules.label: Samsung Klimatechnik — WPLW-Split package label (no extractable component codes)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: bafa_pattern_analysis_only: 'Split' keyword in BAFA product name indicates split-type heat pump system. No extractable ODU/IDU model codes in the registration name. Classification as variant_label (package bundle label). Capped at 0.85.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Samsung WPLW-Split product family
evidence.what_it_proves: 4 BAFA products: WPLW-Hub Split HT R32-12-200-3A, WPLW-Hub Split HT R32-16-200-3A, WPLW-Hydro Split HT R32-12-160-3AI, WPLW-Hydro Split HT R32-16-160-3A. The 'Split' keyword confirms split heat pump system but no individual ODU or IDU model codes are embedded in the name. Registered as complete system bundle labels.

rules.label: Bosch — paren-plus split set bundle SKU (ODU + IDU)
rules.idu_label: AWEi/AWMi indoor unit or CS[n]800iAW indoor

evidence.title: Bosch Compress CS3800iAW/CS5800iAW/CS6800iAW/CS8800iAW product documentation
evidence.what_it_proves: Official Bosch documentation confirms bundle SKUs embed component codes in trailing parenthetical: 'Bundle Name (outdoor_model + indoor_module)'. AWEi = E-series indoor, AWMi = M-series indoor. Outdoor unit always listed first in paren-plus.

rules.example_model: Compress CS5800iAW 4 ORE-S (AW 4 OR-S + CS5800iAW 12 E)

rules.label: Bosch — standalone outdoor units (no paren-plus)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only pattern for standalone classification; BSH-001 external validation partially supports this by confirming that bundle products always have paren-plus suffix.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Bosch standalone outdoor units
evidence.what_it_proves: Bosch Compress CS 5000 AW, CS 6800 AW, CS 7000i, CS 7001i, CS 7400i, CS3400i AWS, and Supraeco SAS series without trailing '(ODU + IDU)' parenthetical are standalone outdoor units. Consistent across all 44 registrations.

rules.label: CLIVET EDGE — 3-component set (EDGE + IDU form / WiSAN + HQCN)

evidence.title: CLIVET official system documentation — EDGE Evo 2.0 + WiSAN + HQCN-NEE architecture
evidence.what_it_proves: CLIVET official documentation confirms EDGE Evo 2.0 is the system controller; WiSAN is the outdoor unit; HQCN-NEE is the indoor hydrobox. The '/' separates the system name from component codes; the '+' between WiSAN and HQCN separates ODU from IDU. All 122 BAFA entries follow this pattern.

rules.example_model: EDGE Evo 2.0 + Box / WiSAN-YME 1 S 2.1 + HQCN-NEE 1 BC A

rules.label: CLIVET EDGE — packaged WiSAN unit (EDGE / WiSAN, no HQCN) — standalone monobloc

evidence.title: CLIVET official documentation — EDGE Evo 2.0 is a product LINE NAME, not a physical indoor unit
evidence.what_it_proves: CLIVET official documentation confirms 'EDGE Evo 2.0' is the system controller/branding line name. Without a HQCN-NEE indoor hydrobox code, the WiSAN registration is a packaged monobloc outdoor unit. The '/' is the system-name-to-component-code separator, not an ODU/IDU pair separator. Compare CLI-001 which explicitly includes HQCN indoor unit code.

rules.false_positive_notes: PREVIOUSLY MISCLASSIFIED as confirmed_set. Reclassified 2026-06-21: EDGE Evo 2.0 without HQCN = monobloc WiSAN outdoor unit only, no separate indoor unit registered.

rules.label: CLIVET Sphera EVO — plus-separator set (SQKN + MiSAN)

evidence.title: CLIVET Sphera EVO 2.0 system documentation — SQKN + MiSAN architecture
evidence.what_it_proves: CLIVET official documentation confirms Sphera EVO 2.0 as a split system with SQKN indoor hydrobox and MiSAN outdoor unit. Plain '+' separator (not '/' + '+' as in EDGE). All 22 BAFA registrations follow this pattern.

rules.example_model: Sphera EVO 2.0 Box SQKN-YEE 1 BC + MiSAN-YEE 1 S 2.1

rules.label: CLIVET — standalone outdoor units (WSAN-*/WiSAN-P/YEE1/YSE1/PMP)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: unvalidated. Some WiSAN-P variants might represent complete packaged units.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — CLIVET standalone WiSAN/WSAN units
evidence.what_it_proves: CLIVET WSAN-YES/YMi/YSC4 and WiSAN-P/YEE1/YSE1/PMP registered without inline IDU code. Likely paired with separate indoor equipment not named in the BAFA registration. External validation not performed.

rules.false_positive_notes: Lower confidence (0.80) because some WiSAN-P variants might be complete packaged units. Flag for manual review.

rules.label: Vaillant — aroTHERM Split/perform set (VWL AS + VWL IS)
rules.idu_label: VWL IS / Hydraulikstation / uniTOWER indoor module
rules.odu_label: VWL AS / aroTHERM outdoor split unit

evidence.title: Vaillant aroTHERM Split official documentation — AS/IS notation
evidence.what_it_proves: Official Vaillant documentation confirms AS = Außenstation (outdoor station) and IS = Innenstation (indoor station). Hydraulikstation and uniTOWER are indoor module variants. Plus-separator in BAFA name confirmed as [outdoor AS] + [indoor IS].

rules.example_model: aroTHERM Split plus VWL 35/8.2 (AS/230V/S2) + Hydraulikstation VWL 57/8.2 IS

rules.label: Vaillant — flexoCOMPACT/flexoTHERM set (+ aroCOLLECT)
rules.idu_label: flexoCOMPACT/flexoTHERM VWF indoor heat pump unit (position 1 in BAFA name)
rules.odu_label: aroCOLLECT VWL outdoor air-source collector (position 2 in BAFA name)

evidence.title: Vaillant flexoCOMPACT/flexoTHERM official documentation — aroCOLLECT pairing
evidence.what_it_proves: Official Vaillant documentation confirms flexoCOMPACT/flexoTHERM VWF is the indoor heat pump unit; aroCOLLECT VWL is the outdoor air-source collector module. Role order noted: VWF=indoor, aroCOLLECT=outdoor collector.

rules.example_model: flexoCOMPACT exclusive VWF 58/4 + aroCOLLECT VWL 11/4 SA
rules.notes: BAFA name order is IDU first, ODU second (flexoCOMPACT + aroCOLLECT). Uses plus_separator_idu_first extractor which reverses the default plus_separator assignment. Fixed in registry v2.1.0 — prior v2.0.0 had idu/odu roles swapped.

rules.label: Vaillant — aroTHERM plus single outdoor unit (no +)

evidence.title: Vaillant aroTHERM plus official documentation — single outdoor unit without indoor module
evidence.what_it_proves: Vaillant documentation confirms aroTHERM plus (single outdoor unit) registered without '+'. The '(A/S2)' or '(A/400V)' in the name are option codes, not IDU/ODU separators.

rules.label: Vaillant — VWL outdoor unit (aroTHERM pro, recoCOMPACT, versoTHERM, standalone VWL)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: self_describing: VWL = Vaillant Wärmepumpe Luft (air-source heat pump) outdoor unit. Products without '+' are registered without a paired indoor unit in this BAFA entry. model_exclude prevents overlap with VAI-001 (aroTHERM Split with + separator). Applied after VAI-001/VAI-002/VAI-003. Confidence 0.88.

evidence.title: Vaillant VWL product naming — VWL = Vaillant Wärmepumpe Luft (outdoor air-source heat pump)
evidence.what_it_proves: Vaillant uses VWL (Vaillant Wärmepumpe Luft) as the product code for outdoor air-source heat pump units across multiple product families: aroTHERM pro, recoCOMPACT exclusive, versoTHERM plus, and standalone VWL. Products without '+' are registered as single outdoor units (monoblock or standalone). The '/N.N' notation in some models (e.g. VWL 115/7.1) is a capacity/generation variant code, not an ODU/IDU separator.

rules.notes: 10 products: 3 aroTHERM pro VWL, 3 recoCOMPACT exclusive VWL, 3 versoTHERM plus VWL, 1 VWL 55/8.2 AS with Hydraulic Station. Applied after VAI-003 (aroTHERM plus/perform). model_exclude '+' prevents overlap with VAI-001. Variant '/N.N' codes in product names (VWL 115/7.1) are correctly not treated as set separators (consistent with DAI-001 and MIT-001 slash=variant policy). The 'with Hydraulic Station' product is classified as confirmed_not_set since the VWL outdoor unit is the primary BAFA registration subject.

rules.label: INVENTOR — ATS outdoor / HU indoor split (slash separator)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: ManualsLib source: one extract described ATS as 'wired controller' while acoustic databook placed ATS outdoors. Resolved by weight of evidence (ATS=outdoor, HU=indoor) but confidence capped at 0.88 due to ambiguity.

evidence.title: ManualsLib: INVENTOR ATS Installation Manual — ATS outdoor / HU indoor
evidence.what_it_proves: Installation manual and product acoustic databook confirm ATS is outdoor unit (acoustics measured at outdoor unit position) and HU is indoor hydraulic unit. One ManualsLib extract described ATS as 'wired controller' creating ambiguity; resolved by weight of evidence from databook.

rules.label: INVENTOR — ATM/ATMH/XFMH/MX monoblock units (no slash)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: capped at 0.85. INV-001 external validation partially supports (ATS=split implies ATM=monoblock) but ATM series not independently confirmed via external source.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — INVENTOR ATM/ATMH monoblock prefix
evidence.what_it_proves: INVENTOR ATM (Air-To-water Mono) and ATMH series have no '/' separator and follow monoblock naming convention. INV-001 (ATS=split) implicitly supports ATM as monoblock by naming contrast.

rules.label: Johnson Controls Hitachi — airH2O RAS+RWM/RWD split set
rules.idu_label: RWM/RWD indoor module (RWM = water module, RWD = DHW combo module)

evidence.title: Hitachi Aircon Documentation Portal: RAS-WH(V)RP2E — Outdoor Units
evidence.what_it_proves: Official Hitachi documentation portal explicitly categorizes RAS-1.5/2/2.5/3WHVRP2E etc. under 'Outdoor Units'.

evidence.title: Hitachi Aircon Documentation Portal: RWM-(N/R)3E — Indoor Units
evidence.what_it_proves: Official Hitachi documentation portal explicitly categorizes RWM-1.5R3E through RWM-10.0N3E under 'Indoor Units'.

evidence.title: ManualsLib: Hitachi airH2O 600 Series Installation and Operation Manual
evidence.what_it_proves: Manual states: 'Split system – Single water module (Indoor unit)' for RWM models. Outdoor units listed as RAS-1.5/2.5/3/5/6/8/10WHVRP2E. RWD is indoor DHW combo module variant. Directly validates split architecture.

evidence.title: ManualsLib: Hitachi RAS-1.5WHVRP2E Installation Manual — Product Guide p.13
evidence.what_it_proves: Product guide labels RAS-1.5/2/2.5/3WHVRP2E as 'Outdoor unit (Split air system)'. Unambiguous direct validation.

rules.label: Johnson Controls Hitachi — Yutaki M monoblock (no +)

evidence.title: Hitachi Air Conditioning UK: Yutaki M official product page
evidence.what_it_proves: Hitachi official page states verbatim: 'The Yutaki M range is a monobloc system, which means it consists only of a compact outdoor unit.' RASM models are single outdoor units with no separate indoor unit.

rules.label: Salvador Escoda — MAB plus HR plus-separator set
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: capped at 0.85. No external Salvador Escoda documentation was consulted to confirm MAB=outdoor and HR=indoor role assignment.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Salvador Escoda MAB+HR notation
evidence.what_it_proves: Salvador Escoda split systems consistently registered as 'MAB-[model] + HR-[model]'. MAB prefix likely = Modulo Aire-Bomba (outdoor air module); HR = Hydronic unit with Reservoir. Pattern consistent across 24 registrations. No external URL validation performed.

rules.label: Midea Europe — MHA plus HB plus-separator set
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: capped at 0.85. No external Midea Europe documentation was consulted to confirm MHA=outdoor and HB=indoor role assignment.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Midea M-Thermal MHA+HB notation
evidence.what_it_proves: Midea Europe M-Thermal split series consistently registered as 'MHA-[model] + HB-[model]'. MHA = outdoor air-source heat pump; HB = indoor Hydraulic Buffer module. Pattern consistent across 22 registrations. No external URL validation performed.

rules.label: NIBE — NIBE SPLIT paren-plus set (AMS + HK)

evidence.title: NIBE official installation manual — AMS 10 outdoor module / HK 200S indoor module
evidence.what_it_proves: NIBE official manual titles: 'Outdoor module AMS 10-8' (outdoor) and 'Inner unit cooperating with air heat pumps HK 200S' (indoor). NIBE SPLIT BAFA names '(AMS 10-8 + HK 200S)' directly match these component names.

rules.label: NIBE — standalone outdoor unit (AMS / F2040 / F2050 / F2120 series)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: manufacturer_official_family_confirmed: AMS 10 outdoor module confirmed by NIBE installation manual; F2040/F2050/F2120 confirmed outdoor air-source units from NIBE EU product catalog. model_exclude prevents overlap with NIB-001 (NIBE SPLIT sets). Confidence 0.90.

evidence.title: NIBE official installation manual — AMS 10 outdoor module (standalone)
evidence.what_it_proves: NIBE installation manual title: 'Outdoor module AMS 10-8' confirming AMS 10 is a standalone outdoor module. F2040/F2050/F2120 are confirmed by NIBE EU product catalog as outdoor air-source heat pumps (Luft/Vatten outdoor split units). All are standalone outdoor units that pair with separate indoor modules when forming a SPLIT set — but BAFA registers them individually here.

rules.notes: model_exclude prevents overlap with NIB-001. NIBE SPLIT paren registrations (2 products) are captured by NIB-001; this rule captures standalone AMS/F-series BAFA registrations (~16 products). AMS 20 = higher-capacity variant of AMS 10 outdoor module family.

rules.label: Remeha — Elga Ace Split IDU+ODU (text label, no extractable codes)

evidence.title: BAFA registration name contains literal 'IDU+ODU' label
evidence.what_it_proves: Remeha Elga Ace Split registered with literal text 'IDU+ODU' in the BAFA name confirming split set. Individual component model codes are NOT embedded — the name is a product bundle label only.

rules.label: Dimplex — System M compound set products
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: capped at 0.85. Compound model codes are opaque; no external Dimplex documentation validated.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Dimplex System M compound products
evidence.what_it_proves: Dimplex 'System M' products are compound heat pump + ventilation systems. The compound model codes embed both components but the encoding is opaque (e.g. '121HCIAOC6'). Classification as set is inferred from product name semantics. No external URL validation performed.

rules.label: Stiebel Eltron — WPL Set keyword

evidence.title: BAFA registration name contains literal 'Set' keyword
evidence.what_it_proves: Only 2 BAFA registrations: 'WPL 19 (I/ IK/ A/ SR/ dB) Set' and 'WPL 24 (I/ IK/ A/ SR/ dB) Set'. The word 'Set' is explicitly included, confirming multi-component package. The '(I/ IK/ A/ SR/ dB)' are unit type variants, not IDU/ODU codes.

rules.example_model: WPL 19 (I/ IK/ A/ SR/ dB) Set

rules.label: Stiebel Eltron — WPL-A outdoor monoblock (Außenaufstellung)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: self_describing: WPL-A = Wärmepumpe Luft Außen (outdoor air heat pump). Designation directly indicates outdoor-installed monoblock. model_exclude prevents overlap with STI-001.

evidence.title: BAFA model name contains WPL-A / WPL A — Außenaufstellung (outdoor installation) designation
evidence.what_it_proves: Stiebel Eltron uses 'WPL-A' (Wärmepumpe Luft Außen) or 'WPL A' to designate outdoor-installed air-to-water heat pump monoblock units. '-A' suffix is Stiebel's standard abbreviation for Außen (outdoor). The model is an outdoor unit with no separate indoor component registered under the same BAFA entry. Confidence 0.92: self-describing designation. model_exclude prevents re-capturing the 2 'Set' products.

rules.notes: Applied after STI-001 (Set keyword). ~18 WPL-A products in 2026-06 snapshot. WPL-I (Innenaufstellung = indoor installed) excluded by pattern specificity.

rules.label: Xtherma — Flex-System/Kombi-System/Pro-System (set name indicators)

evidence.title: Xtherma manufacturer documentation — System product family naming
evidence.what_it_proves: External manufacturer documentation confirms Xtherma Kombi-System = combined multi-component set; Flex-System = flexible split configuration. Confidence upgraded from 0.78 to 0.88 following manufacturer validation. No individual component codes extractable from BAFA name.

rules.label: Mitsubishi Electric — slash = config/region variant (not ODU/IDU)

evidence.title: ManualsLib: Mitsubishi Ecodan Installation Manual — Unit Compatibility (page 8)
evidence.what_it_proves: Official installation manual explicitly lists PUHZ as outdoor unit and EHST/ERSC/ERST as indoor cylinder units. '/' in model codes (e.g., EHST20C-*M*HB/*M*B) separates variants of the same unit type, not indoor/outdoor pairs.

evidence.title: EasyHeatPumps PI Sheet: PUHZ-SW50-120VKA/VHA(-BS)
evidence.what_it_proves: Single PI Sheet covers all four variants VKA/VHA/VKA-BS/VHA-BS of the same outdoor unit range. '/' separates variant subgroups of the same outdoor unit, not indoor/outdoor pairs.

evidence.title: ShopClima: Ecodan Split System ERSC-VM2B + PUHZ-SHW80VHA set notation
evidence.what_it_proves: Mitsubishi Ecodan split sets use '+' notation (ERSC-VM2B + PUHZ-SHW80VHA), not '/'. This proves that '/' in BAFA names like 'PUHZ-SHW80VHA/PUHZ-SHW80VHA-BS' cannot be a set separator.

rules.label: Mitsubishi Electric — ECODAN plus-separator split package (ODU + IDU)

evidence.title: ShopClima: Ecodan Split System — ERSC-VM2B + PUHZ-SHW80VHA set notation (confirmed ODU/IDU roles)
evidence.what_it_proves: Mitsubishi ECODAN split set uses ' + ' notation: indoor cylinder/hydrobox (ERS/EHS prefix) + outdoor unit (PUHZ/PUZ prefix). Role order in BAFA BAFA names is [ODU] + [IDU]: PUD/PUZ/PUHZ/SUZ code before ' + ', EHS/ERS/EHP/ERP code after ' + '. Cross-references MIT-001 evidence: '/' = variant, '+' = set separator. Confidence 0.95.

rules.notes: Applied after MIT-001 (slash=variant_label). All 713 Mitsubishi BAFA products with ' + ' (space-plus-space) are split packages. ODU families: PUD-SHWM/SWM, PUHZ-SHW/SW, PUZ-SHWM/SWM/WM/WZ/HWM, SUZ-SWM. IDU families: EHSD/EHSC/EHST (cylinder indoor), ERSD/ERSC/ERST/ERST (hydrobox), ERPT/EHPT/ERPX (pump type). The 1 model with both '/' and '+' is already captured by MIT-001.

rules.label: Mitsubishi Electric — PUHZ-W standalone packaged/monobloc outdoor unit
rules.confidence_cap_applied: self_describing: PUHZ-W designation per Mitsubishi technical documentation indicates Packaged/Monobloc outdoor unit (W = without separate indoor unit). model_exclude prevents overlap with MIT-001/MIT-002.

evidence.title: Manufacturer technical report — PUHZ-W = Packaged / Monobloc outdoor main unit
evidence.what_it_proves: Mitsubishi ECODAN PUHZ-W series (PUHZ-W60VAA, PUHZ-W85(V)YAA, PUHZ-W112(V)YAA) are registered as standalone outdoor packaged/monobloc units with no separate indoor unit in this BAFA entry. The '-W' designation indicates a packaged outdoor configuration where the refrigerant cycle is self-contained outdoors.

rules.notes: Only 3 BAFA products: PUHZ-W60VAA, PUHZ-W85(V)YAA, PUHZ-W112(V)YAA. Applied after MIT-001 (slash) and MIT-002 (plus). PUHZ-W contains 'PUHZ-W' but NOT 'PUHZ-SW' or 'PUHZ-SHW' (different prefix characters). model_exclude guards against + and / products already handled by earlier rules.

rules.label: Mitsubishi Electric — PUZ-WZ standalone split outdoor unit (registered without IDU)

evidence.title: Manufacturer technical report — PUZ-WZ outdoor split unit, registered standalone in BAFA
evidence.what_it_proves: Mitsubishi PUZ-WZ series appears both as standalone outdoor unit (PUZ-WZ100VAA(-BS)) and paired with EHP/ERP indoor units (PUZ-WZ100VAA(-BS) + EHPT20X-YM9E). The 6 standalone BAFA entries register the outdoor split unit without a paired indoor unit in the same registration. '-BS' = Bivalent System option code.

rules.notes: 6 standalone products: PUZ-WZ50/60/80/85/100/120 (VAA/YAA) (-BS) variants. Applied after MIT-001/MIT-002. model_exclude for '+' prevents overlap with MIT-002 (PUZ-WZ products that appear paired with EHP/ERP indoor units).

rules.label: Daikin — slash = tank size, antifreeze variant, or wiring option

evidence.title: Daikin EU product page: EHBH-E6V / ERGA04-08EVA — indoor and outdoor roles
evidence.what_it_proves: Daikin's own product page labels EHBH-E6V as indoor unit and ERGA04-08EVA as outdoor unit. In BAFA entries like 'EHBH08D6V/EHBH08D6W', both codes begin with EHBH (same unit family) — confirming '/' separates variants of the same indoor unit, not an indoor/outdoor pair.

evidence.title: Daikin UK: EHBH-D6V product page — D6V/D9W as variant suffixes
evidence.what_it_proves: EHBH-D6V is the indoor wall-hung unit; related outdoor unit is ERGA-DV3. D6V and D9W suffixes both appear in the EHBH indoor unit specification table — confirming D6V/D9W are wiring/phase variants of the same indoor unit.

evidence.title: Daikin EU: ETSH-D ECH2O floor-standing — P30/P50 tank size variants
evidence.what_it_proves: ECH2O models use P30 and P50 to distinguish tank sizes (ETSH16P30DA vs ETSH16P50DA). Confirms 'Altherma 3 HT ECH2O 180l/230l' uses '/' to separate tank-size variants of the same product, not components.

rules.label: Wolf — CHC/CHT-MONOBLOCK explicit (inactive; superseded by WLF-002)

evidence.title: BAFA registration name contains literal 'MONOBLOCK' keyword
evidence.what_it_proves: Wolf CHC-MONOBLOCK and CHT-MONOBLOCK series have the word 'MONOBLOCK' literally in the BAFA model name. No ambiguity.

rules.notes: Root cause of prior zero-hit diagnosis was manufacturer_contains case mismatch ('Wolf' vs actual 'WOLF GmbH'). Pattern CHC-MONOBLOCK|CHT-MONOBLOCK is correct and would match 20 BAFA products. Kept inactive; WLF-002 (contains_any MONOBLOCK) is the active replacement with a simpler pattern.

rules.label: Wolf — CHC/CHT-MONOBLOCK self-describing outdoor monoblock
rules.confidence_cap_applied: self_describing: MONOBLOCK literal present in BAFA name

evidence.title: BAFA registration name contains literal 'MONOBLOCK' keyword
evidence.what_it_proves: Wolf CHC-MONOBLOCK and CHT-MONOBLOCK series have the word 'MONOBLOCK' literally in the BAFA model name. No indoor unit. Classification is self-certifying.

rules.notes: manufacturer_contains uses 'WOLF' (all caps) to match actual BAFA field 'WOLF GmbH'. Simpler than WLF-001 regex; covers all 20 CHC/CHT-MONOBLOCK products via contains_any.

rules.label: Wolf — CHA/FHA capacity range slash (not ODU/IDU, not MONOBLOCK)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only pattern: capped at 0.88

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Wolf CHA/FHA capacity range slash
evidence.what_it_proves: Wolf CHA and FHA product codes use '/' to indicate capacity range steps (e.g. CHA-16/20-400V = 16 kW or 20 kW capacity, FHA-08/10 = 8 kW or 10 kW). Both segments share the same prefix, confirming this is a capacity variant, not ODU/IDU separation. Pattern is self-evident from model code structure.

rules.notes: Renamed from WLF-002; manufacturer_contains corrected from 'Wolf' to 'WOLF' to match actual BAFA field. model_exclude updated to contains_any MONOBLOCK (simpler than prior CHC-MONOBLOCK|CHT-MONOBLOCK regex). bafa_count_approx updated 9→15 (prior count was wrong due to manufacturer case mismatch giving 0 actual hits).

rules.label: ELCO — slash = variant name within single registration

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — ELCO AEROTOP slash variant naming
evidence.what_it_proves: ELCO AEROTOP uses '/' to register multiple variant names (e.g. INOX / INOX OPTIC, -RXL / -CRX 1Z / -CRX 2Z) and 2-part installation notes under one BAFA entry. Pattern is self-evident: slash segments share the same AEROTOP base model name.

rules.label: Weishaupt — slash = capacity range or variant suffix

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Weishaupt WWP slash variant
evidence.what_it_proves: Weishaupt WWP products use '/' for capacity ranges (WEB 10/15-A = 10 or 15 kW) or recovery option variants (AD / ADR where ADR = AD + Recovery). Both cases are self-evident variant patterns, not ODU/IDU separators.

rules.label: Weishaupt — WAB outdoor monoblock (Wärmepumpe Außen Blockheizkraftwerk)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: self_describing: WAB = Wärmepumpe Außen Block, indicating outdoor-installed monoblock heat pump unit. Confidence 0.92.

evidence.title: BAFA model name contains WAB — Wärmepumpe Außen Block (outdoor monoblock) designation
evidence.what_it_proves: Weishaupt WAB (Wärmepumpe Außen Block) = outdoor air-source heat pump monoblock. The 'A' (Außen = outdoor) and 'B' (Block = compact block unit) designators unambiguously indicate a standalone outdoor monoblock installation with no separate indoor unit registered. ~4 WAB products in 2026-06 snapshot.

rules.notes: WEI-001 (slash variant_label) is not excluded because WAB products do not contain '/'. The rules are evaluated in order; WAB products are not slash-containing so WEI-001 does not match them.

rules.label: GD TCL — SMKL indoor / THF outdoor split set
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: capped at 0.85. No external GD TCL documentation was consulted to confirm SMKL=indoor and THF=outdoor role assignment.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — GD TCL SMKL/THF notation
evidence.what_it_proves: GD TCL registers split systems as 'SMKL-[indoor model] / THF-[outdoor model]'. Space-slash-space separator consistent across 88 registrations. SMKL appears to be the indoor controller; THF the outdoor heat pump unit. Role assignment is inferred from prefix ordering and pattern structure — no external validation performed.

rules.label: GD TCL — HB[n]SP0/TP0 monoblock model codes
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: capped at 0.85. No external GD TCL documentation consulted.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — GD TCL HB monoblock codes
evidence.what_it_proves: GD TCL standalone monoblock units are registered with simple HB[capacity][S/T]P0 codes. S = single-phase, T = three-phase. No separator characters. Pattern consistent across 26 registrations.

rules.label: Germany GREE — GRS-CQ confirmed split set (slash = refrigerant/variant suffix within single code, no extractable component codes)
rules.idu_label: split indoor unit (codes not separable from BAFA registration)
rules.odu_label: split outdoor unit (codes not separable from BAFA registration)

evidence.title: GREE GRS-CQ Service Manual and Split-VERSATI-Submittal documentation
evidence.what_it_proves: GREE service manual and 'Split-VERSATI-Submittal' documentation confirm GRS-CQ series are genuine SPLIT systems with separate outdoor and indoor units. The '/' in model codes like 'GRS-CQ10Pd/NhH-M' is a refrigerant-class/noise-suffix separator WITHIN a single combined model code, not an IDU/ODU pair separator. The combined code represents one BAFA registration for a complete split system.

rules.false_positive_notes: PREVIOUSLY MISCLASSIFIED as variant_label. Reclassified 2026-06-21: GRS-CQ IS a split system (service manual confirmed), but no individual IDU/ODU model codes can be extracted from the BAFA name. The '/' is a model-code-internal separator, not an outdoor/indoor separator.

rules.label: AUX/Ningbo — slash within single model code (refrigerant class suffix)

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Ningbo AUX ACHP-H refrigerant suffix slash
evidence.what_it_proves: Ningbo AUX ACHP-H series encode refrigerant class and variant after '/' within a single model code (e.g. ACHP-H04/4R2HA-M where 4R2 = R32/R410A variant). The post-slash segment is a refrigerant code, not a component model.

rules.label: FläktGroup — embedded '+' in model code (not set separator)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: capped at 0.85.

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — FläktGroup FGAH embedded plus option code
evidence.what_it_proves: FläktGroup FGAH product codes embed '+' as a product-option indicator within a single model string (e.g. FGAH2020AG1.SL+.E79 where '+' signals an added option package). The '+' appears between model suffix fragments, not between two independent model codes.

rules.label: Enpal — EODU prefix (standalone outdoor unit)

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Enpal EODU prefix
evidence.what_it_proves: Enpal registers products with 'EODU' (Enpal Outdoor Unit) prefix. The literal 'ODU' in the prefix indicates outdoor-only registration. Paired indoor unit may be a separate BAFA entry or not registered. No external validation performed.

rules.label: Carrier — multi-unit commercial (requires research)

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — Carrier 30RQ multi-component commercial
evidence.what_it_proves: Carrier 30RQ commercial heat pump registrations include '+' separating unit codes (e.g. '30RQ-060R + 15LS + 119D'). Structure appears to be a multi-unit commercial system with 3+ components. Requires product documentation research to confirm component roles.

rules.label: Johnson Controls Systems — York YKF plus-separator split set
rules.idu_label: YKF indoor module (DHW tank integration suffix)
rules.confidence_cap_applied: BAFA-only: capped at 0.85; actual value 0.82 due to model code pattern ambiguity (both outdoor and indoor share YKF prefix, role determination by code structure only).

evidence.title: BAFA master seed pattern analysis — York YKF outdoor/indoor code structure
evidence.what_it_proves: Johnson Controls Systems registers York YKF heat pumps as 'YKF[outdoor capacity]ANB + YKF[indoor size][tank]ANB***'. The '+' separates outdoor from indoor module with optional DHW tank (060L, 100L, 190L, 240L). The '***' suffix = variant placeholder. Not external-documentation-validated.
